question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R2 adds an IS-IS interface-level HMAC-MD5 hello-password on the R2↔R3
link (GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0). R3 has no password configured. R3
rejects R2's authenticated Hellos as malformed; R2 rejects R3's
unauthenticated Hellos for missing the auth TLV. The Level-2 adjacency
drops on the primary path. SPF recomputes around the broken adjacency;
R2 immediately promotes its backup interface (Gi0/0/0/1 to R6) — but
loses TI-LFA fast-reroute on Gi0/0/0/0 (no adjacency to back up).
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.102 (R2), an IS-IS HMAC-MD5 hello-password (secret123) was added to GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R2<->R3 primary link) while R3 has none.
The two ends disagree on Hello authentication: R2 expects an authenticated Hello with a valid auth TLV while R3 sends none, and R3 expects an unauthenticated Hello while R2 sends an authenticated one, so each end rejects the other's IIHs and the Level-2 adjacency on the primary path cannot stay up. IS-IS therefore reconverges onto the backup R2->R6->R7->R4 path, and TI-LFA protection on Gi0/0/0/0 is lost because there is no adjacency left to back up.
Fix: remove the hello-password from R2's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0.
expected_symptoms_summary:
%ROUTING-ISIS-5-AUTH_FAILURE_DROP reported by R2 (1 instance):
- R2: ISIS (LAB): Dropped P2P IIH from Gi0/0/0/0, R3 (0000.0000.0003), SNPA aac1.abd6.aaa1 due to authentication TLV not found

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 hello-password hmac-md5 clear secret123

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 no hello-password

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:13:39.722 UTC: isis[1003]: %ROUTING-ISIS-5-AUTH_FAILURE_DROP : ISIS (LAB): Dropped P2P IIH from Gi0/0/0/0, R3 (0000.0000.0003), SNPA aac1.abd6.aaa1 due to authentication TLV not found
